cases.title: Successful registration transaction preserves capacity invariant without double booking
preconditions: Event is RegistrationOpen with capacity N and available seats
Participant has no active registration for the event
Postgres database via Compose db service with migrations applied
steps: Invoke registrationService.register(eventId, participantId, actorContext) inside a committed transaction
Query Registered seat-holder count and active registration rows for the participant/event pair
cases.expected: Registration commits with state Registered; INV-02 holds (Registered count <= capacity); INV-01 holds (at most one active registration per participant/event); no duplicate seat assignment

cases.title: Check-in transaction atomically persists CheckinRecord and registration CheckedIn state
preconditions: Event is InProgress with active check-in window
Participant has Registered registration with no prior valid check-in
Staff or admin actor authorized for event check-in
steps: Invoke checkinService.staffCheckin(eventId, { registrationId }, actorContext)
Query checkin_records and registration state for the registrationId
cases.expected: Single valid CheckinRecord with timestamp exists; registration state is CheckedIn; INV-04 holds (at most one valid check-in per registration); registration and check-in rows are mutually consistent

cases.title: Flow A register-then-check-in maintains data consistency end-to-end
preconditions: E2E Flow A fixture: published event with open registration then InProgress with check-in window
Participant JWT with no prior registration
Staff JWT for check-in
steps: POST /api/v1/events/{eventId}/registrations to obtain Registered state
POST /api/v1/events/{eventId}/checkins with registrationId during active check-in window
GET registration-status and attendance for the participant
cases.expected: Exactly one active registration exists throughout; after check-in exactly one valid check-in record with matching checkinAt; registration state CheckedIn; no orphaned or duplicate booking artifacts

cases.title: Registration acceptance uses transactional row lock and in-transaction seat count
preconditions: Event with capacity 3 and multiple concurrent registration attempts
lockEventRuleConfig (or equivalent) serializes writers per event
Postgres as system of record per docker-compose local runtime policy
steps: Fire parallel registrationService.register calls from distinct participants
Inspect database transaction boundaries and post-commit Registered count
cases.expected: Each registration path runs inside BEGIN/COMMIT with row lock on event rule config; seat count computed in-transaction; no committed state where Registered count exceeds capacity

cases.title: Cancellation and waitlist promotion run atomically without transient double booking
preconditions: Event at capacity with Registered participants and at least one Waitlisted
waitlistEnabled true
One Registered participant eligible to cancel before deadline
steps: Cancel a Registered registration triggering FIFO promotion in the same transaction
Query Registered count and waitlist positions before and after commit
cases.expected: Promotion and cancellation commit atomically; Registered count never exceeds capacity at any observable point; no intermediate state with two promotions consuming one seat or zero Registered when capacity allows one

cases.title: Domain invariants INV-01, INV-02, and INV-04 hold after mixed registration and check-in sequence
preconditions: Database reset per test with registration and check-in modules enabled
Mix of register, duplicate-block, cancel, promote, and check-in operations on seeded events
steps: Execute a deterministic sequence covering Flow A and Flow B partial paths
After each committed operation verify INV-01 (one active registration per participant/event), INV-02 (Registered <= capacity), INV-04 (one valid check-in per registration)
cases.expected: All three invariants hold after every commit; aligns with system overview consistency goal and testing plan P0 severity for capacity overflow and duplicate active registration

cases.title: Heavy concurrent registration burst never produces double booking or duplicate active rows
preconditions: Event RegistrationOpen with capacity 3 and waitlistEnabled true
12+ unique participantIds with no prior registration
Parallel registrationService.register calls fired simultaneously
steps: Execute Promise.allSettled on all concurrent register calls
Count Registered rows, active registrations per participant, and compare to capacity
cases.expected: Registered count is at most 3 and equals 3 when all attempts resolve; each participant has at most one active registration; zero capacity overflow and zero duplicate active registrations under contention

cases.title: Concurrent check-in attempts for same registration yield at most one valid check-in
preconditions: Registered participant with no prior check-in
Event InProgress with active check-in window
Two or more parallel staffCheckin calls targeting the same registrationId
steps: Fire concurrent checkinService.staffCheckin calls for the same registrationId
Count valid checkin_records and registration state after all calls settle
cases.expected: Exactly one valid CheckinRecord exists; registration is CheckedIn once; duplicate attempts rejected with conflict error without creating a second valid check-in; INV-04 preserved

cases.title: Failed registration rolls back entirely with no partial seat consumption
preconditions: Registration path that fails post-insert capacity guard (CAPACITY_EXCEEDED) or mid-transaction error
Known Registered count before attempt
steps: Trigger registration that fails after insert attempt or during capacity check
Query Registered count and any new rows for the participant after ROLLBACK
cases.expected: Transaction is fully rolled back; Registered count unchanged; no partial registration row leaves inconsistent capacity or double-booking exposure

cases.title: Rejected out-of-window check-in leaves registration and check-in tables unchanged
preconditions: Registered participant with no check-in
Fixed clock set outside check-in window (before openAt or after closeAt)
steps: Attempt checkinService.staffCheckin for the registration
Query checkin_records count and registration state
cases.expected: Check-in rejected with appropriate error code; zero CheckinRecord rows created; registration remains Registered (not CheckedIn); data consistency preserved on validation failure

cases.title: Concurrent duplicate registration from same participant yields single active row
preconditions: Event RegistrationOpen with available seats
Same participantId used in parallel register calls with no prior registration
steps: Fire two or more concurrent registrationService.register calls for the same participantId and eventId
Count active registration rows for the participant/event pair
cases.expected: Exactly one request succeeds; others rejected with REGISTRATION_DUPLICATE_ACTIVE or equivalent conflict; database never holds more than one active registration; prevents double booking of the same participant slot

cases.title: Transactional consistency tests execute against Compose Postgres not in-memory stores
preconditions: npm run aih:dev:db:up has started healthy db service
DATABASE_URL points to postgresql://we_event:we_event@localhost:5432/we_event
Forbidden persistence modes (in-memory Map, SQLite, mock-only repos) are not used
steps: Run npm run test:integration for registration and check-in consistency scenarios
Verify tests connect to Postgres and use schema migrations
cases.expected: Integration suite exercises real transactional behavior on Postgres per docker-compose local runtime policy; concurrency and atomicity assertions are meaningful against durable storage

cases.title: Flow B cancel-promote-check-in preserves capacity and check-in consistency
preconditions: E2E Flow B fixture: full event (capacity 1), one Registered, one Waitlisted
Event transitions to InProgress with active check-in window after promotion
steps: Registered participant cancels; waitlisted participant promoted to Registered
Promoted participant completes check-in via POST checkins or self-checkin
Verify Registered count and check-in records at each step
cases.expected: Registered count never exceeds capacity through cancel and promotion; promoted participant has exactly one valid check-in after check-in step; no duplicate registrations or check-ins at any stage

cases.title: Registration pipeline validators enforce dedupe before capacity assignment
preconditions: Unit tests for registration dedupe validator and capacity guard validator per testing plan unit targets
steps: Run assertNoDuplicateActive against existing active row → expect REGISTRATION_DUPLICATE_ACTIVE
Run capacity guard with registeredCount >= capacity → expect Waitlisted or rejection, never Registered
cases.expected: Pure validators deterministically enforce ordering that prevents double booking logic errors before persistence; dedupe runs before capacity decision in the validation pipeline
